title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service
impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

updates.content:
Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may be unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and get a "Request policy is null" error.

Current status: We're investigating a potential issue with Microsoft Intune and checking for impact to your organization. We'll provide an update within 30 minutes.

Title: Some users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service

User impact: Users' Microsoft Intune devices may be unable to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service.

More info: Additionally, some admins may be unable to load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade and get a "Request policy is null" error.

Current status: After our analysis of service health telemetry, we suspect that an authentication component has been misconfigured, and we're confirming this is the source of the impact in order to develop a solution to resolve the issue.

Scope of impact: Some users and admins in North America, Europe, Middle East, and Africa attempting to retrieve Intune Management Agent scripts from the service on Microsoft Intune devices or load the Intune Admin console 'Scripts and Remediations' blade may be impacted.

Next update by: Thursday, May 21, 2026, at 5:30 PM UTC
